It says \"missing PCI subdevice entries for 0x2526 (0x04010, 0x4018 and 0x401C)\".Let me think about this differently. Looking at the notation:- \"0x04010\" - this has 5 digits which is unusual. But looking at the drv.c file, I see entries like \"0x4034\", \"0x40A4\", \"0x4234\", \"0x42A4\".- So maybe \"0x04010\" is a typo and should be \"0x0401\", \"0x4010\", or something else?\n\nActually, wait. Let me reconsider. Looking at the existing entries in the iwl_dev_info_table:- 0x0010, 0x0014, 0x0018, 0x001C (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)- 0x6010, 0x6014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x8010, 0x8014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0xA014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0xE010, 0xE014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x0030, 0x0038, 0x003C (iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x4030 (iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg)And in the pci_device_id_list:- 0x4034 (iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg_soc)\n- 0x40A4 (iwl9462_2ac_cfg_soc)\n- 0x4234 (iwl9560_2ac_cfg_soc)\n\n\n- 0x42A4 (iwl9462_2ac_cfg_soc)\n\nSo there are 0x40xx and 0x42xx entries in pci_device_id_list, but the iwl_dev_info_table only has 0x4030. The bug mentions \"0x04010, 0x4018 and 0x401C\". If I interpret this as 0x0401, 0x4018, and 0x401C, I notice a pattern emerging - there's a sequence like 0x0010, 0x0014, 0x0018, 0x001C in the existing table, so maybe the missing entries follow a similar increment pattern.\n\nIf there's a sequence 0x4010, 0x4014, 0x4018, 0x401C that should map to iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg (since the 0x00**/0x10/0x14/0x18/0x1C pattern maps to 9260), these entries are missing from both tables. The notation in the bug report could be ambiguous - perhaps \"0x04010\" is actually \"0x0401\" concatenated with \"0\" due to line wrapping, or it's using a different notation altogether. I should look for documentation or related bugs that might clarify what device IDs are actually expected to be supported.\n\nRunning a test script when available would help confirm what's expected. Based on the patterns observed, device 0x2526 (9260) is missing firmware loading, and the most likely missing entries are 0x4010, 0x4014, 0x4018, 0x401C that should map to iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg. The fix would involve adding these entries to either the pci_device_id_list in drv.c or the iwl_dev_info_table in drv.c.\n\nI need to examine the structure more carefully to determine which one requires updating.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1512,"cache_read_input_tokens":41017,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1512},"output_tokens":5,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d32c6b08-b60a-4fcc-b14e-c255ce8e4d53","uuid":"10f7fb01-0e72-4bc9-9d61-d4223a82a7e8"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01N3c1B2xKtqe7Ex8ATwXhff","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good! Now I understand the structure. There are two tables:\n\n1. `iwl_hw_card_ids[]` - a PCI device ID table that maps (device, subdevice) to a configuration. This is used for initial PCI device matching.\n\n2. `iwl_dev_info_table[]` - a more detailed device info table that maps (device, subdevice) to a configuration and a device name. This seems to be used for more specific device identification.\n\nNow, the bug says that device 0x2526 (9260) with certain subdevice IDs is missing firmware loading. Looking at the entries in iwl_dev_info_table for 0x2526:\n\n- 0x0010, 0x0014, 0x0018, 0x001C (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x6010, 0x6014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x8010, 0x8014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0xA014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)- 0xE010, 0xE014 (iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x0030, 0x0038, 0x003C (iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg)\n- 0x4030 (iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg)\n\nAnd in pci_device_id_list:\n- 0x4034 (iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg_soc)\n- 0x40A4 (iwl9462_2ac_cfg_soc)- 0x4234 (iwl9560_2ac_cfg_soc)\n- 0x42A4 (iwl9462_2ac_cfg_soc)- PCI_ANY_ID (iwl9000_trans_cfg)\n\n\nThe bug specifically mentions \"missing PCI subdevice entries for 0x2526 (0x04010, 0x4018 and 0x401C)\". These subdevice IDs don't appear in either table for device 0x2526, which explains why the firmware isn't loading for those variants. The notation suggests these are distinct entries that need to be added to properly support those hardware configurations. the 0x4030 entry maps to iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg, which breaks the pattern I'd expect from the 0x0030 series. Let me identify which device IDs appear in the PCI list but lack corresponding entries in the device info table. Several IDs like 0x0034, 0x0060, 0x0064, 0x00A0, and 0x00A4 are missing, along with a range starting at 0x0210 through 0x023C.\n\nThere's clearly a fallback system at work—when a specific device/subdevice combination isn't found in the info table, the driver defaults to the PCI device ID list. For the missing subdevice IDs 0x4010, 0x4018, and 0x401C, I need to determine how they're handled by this fallback mechanism.\n\nSince these IDs aren't present in either lookup table, they'd match the PCI_ANY_ID wildcard and get assigned iwl9000_trans_cfg. However, if firmware loading is failing, the device is likely being recognized but mapped to an incorrect configuration rather than not being detected at all. The solution would involve adding proper entries to the device info table to ensure these subdevices get the right configuration.\n\nLooking at the naming patterns in the existing entries, the 0x40** series seems to follow a similar structure to the 0x00** series—0x4010, 0x4018, 0x401C might correspond to the same generation as their 0x00** counterparts. The 0x4030 entry maps to iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg, suggesting the 0x40** prefix might indicate a variant of that same device family. I need to determine whether these newer IDs should map to iwl9260 or iwl9560 configurations based on the actual hardware generation they represent.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":416,"cache_creation_input_tokens":598,"cache_read_input_tokens":44860,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":598},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d32c6b08-b60a-4fcc-b14e-c255ce8e4d53","uuid":"b483869b-960a-42c8-98b4-111ae4c601f7"}
